claimText: Solar flares can cause geomagnetic storms that disturb Earth's magnetic field, leading to radio blackouts, power grid failures, and auroras.
evidenceExcerpt: Solar flares cause geomagnetic storms that can lead to radio blackouts, power grid failures, and auroras.
examUse: Statement-based question on the impacts of space weather on Earth.
distractorHooks: Geomagnetic storms do not affect terrestrial power grids
Auroras are caused by the reflection of sunlight off polar ice

claimText: The solar activity cycle lasts approximately 11 years.
evidenceExcerpt: The solar activity cycle lasts about 11 years
examUse: Statement-based question on the duration of the solar cycle.
distractorHooks: The solar cycle is an annual phenomenon

claimText: The peak of the solar activity cycle, known as the solar maximum, is characterized by frequent solar storms and increased instances of sunspots.
evidenceExcerpt: peaking at the solar maximum with frequent solar storms and sunspots.
examUse: Statement-based question linking solar maximum to sunspot frequency.
distractorHooks: Solar maximum is characterized by a decrease in sunspots
Sunspots only appear during the solar minimum

claimText: The fastest Earth-directed Coronal Mass Ejections (CMEs) can reach Earth in 15 to 18 hours.
evidenceExcerpt: Fastest Earth-directed CMEs can reach Earth in 15-18 hours.
examUse: Statement-based question on the travel time of CMEs to Earth.
distractorHooks: CMEs reach Earth instantly at the speed of light
CMEs take several weeks to travel from the Sun to Earth
